intake.user_request: After Option 1 was ruled and DISPATCH-006 fired (but before CORE accepted), CORE shared a post-Option-1-ruling code review identifying that the architectural fix is to consolidate to one analyzer slice — rag-index reads .coderef/graph.json directly instead of running its own analyzerService.analyze(...). User confirmed the bulletproof framing. ORCHESTRATOR pivoted: pause DISPATCH-006, supersede the analyzer-slice-coverage WO, author this replacement.
intake.superseded_wo: WO-RAG-INDEX-ANALYZER-SLICE-COVERAGE-001 (status=superseded; same dir + git history preserved)
intake.stub_disposition: STUB-RAG-INDEX-ANALYZER-SLICE-COVERAGE-001 closes via supersession — its premise (two analyzer slices need reconciling) is moot once there is one slice.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Direct refactor with known fix shape. Phase 1: investigate read-side staleness check options. Phase 2: replace analyze() with graph.json read + delete facet enrichment block. Phase 3: tests + invariant. Phase 4: dynamic re-verify (AC-09 as identity) + close.
classification.discovery: Code review confirmed indexing-orchestrator.ts:391 calls analyzerService.analyze(patterns, false) independently of populate-coderef. Lines 462-564 read graph.json only for facet enrichment onto chunks already produced from the second scan. Replacing line 391 with a graph.json read makes the facet enrichment block redundant — the chunks come from the graph that carries the facets.

scope.in_scope: (1) Replace analyzerService.analyze(...) at indexing-orchestrator.ts:391 with JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('.coderef/graph.json', 'utf-8')). (2) Convert the parsed JSON to the in-memory DependencyGraph shape ChunkConverter expects (Map-of-nodes + edges array). (3) Delete the facet enrichment block (lines ~462-564) — chunks already carry facets from the graph they were built from. (4) Add stale-graph check: stat graph.json + sample source files; warn or fail if any source mtime > graph mtime. (5) Add explicit error if graph.json missing: 'run coderef populate before coderef rag-index.' (6) Update tests; restore AC-09 as identity.
out_of_scope: Phase 7 archive — locked, must NOT be reopened
Predecessor WO archive (path-normalization-fix) — closed, must NOT be modified
Superseded WO directory (rag-index-analyzer-slice-coverage) — leave files in place; status block on communication.json suffices for audit trail
src/analyzer/* and src/scanner/* changes — populate-coderef side untouched
The 5 other substrate concepts from CORE's bulletproof feedback (frozen fixture, branded paths, schema reduction, seam deletion, two-grain drop) — file as separate stubs, scope as separate WOs

constraints: Phase 7 archive MUST NOT be reopened (rebuild COMPLETE)
Cultural validator scope-mode + tracking validator scope-mode must PASS clean
tsc --noEmit clean both configs after each commit

evidence_summary.ac_09_pre_pivot_state: 215/262 chunks correctly skip (post-path-normalization-fix; 47-file gap due to two analyzer slices)
evidence_summary.ac_09_post_pivot_target: 262/262 by identity (chunksSkipped(header_status_missing) === header_missing_count, exactly — no ±10% band needed)
evidence_summary.structural_proof: Once chunks come from the same graph.json that validation-report counts header_missing from, the two metrics see the same file population by construction. There is no slice for them to disagree about.
code_evidence: indexing-orchestrator.ts:391 — analyzerService.analyze(patterns, false) call to delete
indexing-orchestrator.ts:462-564 — facet enrichment block to delete (graph.json read happens here, but only for enrichment, not as the chunk source)
chunk-converter.ts:214-224 — groupNodesByFile iterates graph.nodes faithfully; whatever graph goes in, ChunkConverter chunks it. The slice is decided entirely upstream.
indexing-orchestrator.ts:40-53 — normalizeChunkFileForGraphJoin stays useful (chunk grain is still chunk grain)
